data.title: Global Blue Group stock slips despite landing Outperform rating from Oppenheimer
data.description:
Global Blue Group Holding AG (NYSE:GB) shares dipped 2% in Thursday midafternoon trading, even after Oppenheimer analyst Rayna Kumar started coverage on the provider of technology and payment solutions with an Outperform rating.

Kumar expects the company to keep benefiting from the ongoing recovery in international travel, as well as improved value-added-tax conversion rates, he wrote in a note.

"Global Blue likely will continue to outpace industry growth, partially due to gaining presence in new geographies and digitization efforts driving greater VAT refund adoption."

The analyst sees a revenue compounded annual growth rate of 14% over the next two years, surpassing Global Blue's (GB) long-term goal of 8%-12%. Over the same period, Kumar expects adjusted EBITDA margin to expand 400 basis points, annually on average, given its high fixed cost structure.

Oppenheimer's Outperform rate deviates from the SA Quant system rating of Hold and aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Strong Buy.

data.title: U.S. companies exposed to the nuclear energy trend
data.description:
BofA Securities listed 23 U.S. companies that are exposed to nuclear energy and could benefit from the bipartisan support to investments and policy measures.

Although Asia is leading investments in clean energy technologies with about 90% of mass-manufacturing capacity, other developed markets, such as the U.S., have introduced measures like the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act that aims at incentivizing companies to re-shore manufacturing of clean tech.

“In the U.S., nuclear investments have bipartisan support, and so face less risk of funding cuts no matter the election outcome,” said Panos Seretis, global head of Sustainability Research at BofA, in a note.

Analysts highlighted companies benefiting from these policy measures. These are 23 U.S.-listed companies that are developing nuclear technologies or that are part of the supply chain from mining uranium to supplying electrical equipment, said Seretis.

Here are the companies, with the percentage of ESG funds – environmental, social and governance; funds that aim at sustainability goals – with the position:

  • Ameren (AEE) – % of ESG Funds with position: 4.3%

data.title: Alibaba cloud, international segments seen as 'bright spots' going into Q3: Baird
data.description:
The cloud and international segments of Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) are seen as "bright spots" for the Chinese tech giant going into the company's third-quarter earnings report, research firm Baird said.

"We expect accelerating cloud segment growth given healthy trends in public cloud adoption and strong demand for AI-related services - higher capex too!" analyst Colin Sebastian wrote in a note to clients. "International segment revenues continue to benefit from AliExpress Choice (cross-border/fast shipping) and the company's investments in marketing to sustain momentum." Sebastian has an Outperform rating and $110 price target on Alibaba.

Meanwhile, the company's e-commerce segment is seen as having had a "mixed" quarter, as trends may have slowed down since July. There was a three to five percentage increase in online sales of physical goods, according to data from China’s National Bureau of Statistics, down from low-teens growth in July.

Data for August and September showed "flattish" year-over-year gains, while cumulative online sales increased roughly 8% year-over-year year-to-date.

"While we continue to view Alibaba as a strong technology platform with meaningful scale benefits, we question the pace of improvement in consumer/online spending in China with ongoing strong competition," Sebastian added.

data.title: Study finds high costs, few benefits associated with GLP-1 weight loss drugs
data.description:
A new study from a pharmacy benefit manager is casting doubt on the benefits of GLP-1 drugs used for weight loss, finding they are associated with a high cost of care with little benefit beyond dropping pounds.

The study from Prime Therapeutics found that individuals who didn't have diabetes that started a GLP-1 for obesity had an average $4,206 higher cost of care in their second year compared to those not taking one of the drugs. In addition, across obesity-related outcomes, no reduction in medical events was seen over two years.

The two most popular GLP-1s for obesity are Novo Nordisk's (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) and Eli Lilly's (NYSE:LLY) Zepbound (tirzepatide).

In addition, those taking GLP-1s not only didn't see a medical cost offset from their treatment over two years but can instead expect a total cost of care of $11,200 at two years given standard drug prices, according to Prime.

Results also showed an increased risk of acute pancreatitis in the first year of therapy with one additional acute pancreatitis case per 250 GLP-1-treated individuals.

"While individuals experience weight loss from taking a GLP-1 drug, in our real-world analysis, no further health benefit is observable after two years, and in fact, they end up with a substantially higher cost of care than those who are not taking the drug," Prime VP, Pharmacy Clinical Services, David Lassen said in a statement.

A Prime report released in July found that most GLP-1 users stop taking the drugs within two years.

data.title: Ingram Micro pops 16% after raising $400M in IPO
data.description:
• Ingram Micro (NYSE:INGM) shares rose 16% and opened at $25.28 on Thursday after the IT solutions provider raised $440M in its initial public offering.
  • The company sold 11.6M shares in the offering priced at $22 each, with a selling shareholder offering 7M shares.
  • For the 26 weeks ended on June 29, Ingram Micro generated $22.87M in revenue. Revenue in 2023 came in at $48.04M, down from the $50.82M generated in 2022.
  • It generated a gross profit of $1.66M during the 29-week period as of June 29, compared to a gross profit of $3.55MM and $3.7M in 2023 and 2022, respectively.
  • As of June 29, 2024, Ingram Micro had $929M in cash, $14.2B in total liabilities, and it had a negative free cash flow of $120.5M for the period.

data.title: ICON leads a selloff among CROs after guidance cut; Baird downgrades
data.description:
ICON plc (NASDAQ:ICLR) traded sharply lower on Thursday, sparking a selloff among its rival contract research organizations after the Ireland-based company cut its guidance with its Q3 results. In reaction, Baird downgraded the stock to neutral from outperform.

Despite a ~20% YoY rise in net income, Icon’s (NASDAQ:ICLR) Q3 results missed Wall Street forecasts as its revenue contracted ~1% YoY to $2.0B.

“ICON’s results for the third quarter did not meet the expectations we had previously provided due to specific customer and division-level impacts,” CEO Steve Cutler remarked.

He attributed the revenue miss to various factors, including cautious R&D activity among biotechs and two large customers facing budget cuts and changes to their development models.

“We expect these impacts to continue into quarter four, and as a result, we are taking decisive action to realign our resources to forecasted activity,” Cutler added.

The company trimmed its full-year adjusted earnings per share guidance by $1.10 on both ends to $13.90-$14.10 and cut its revenue guidance by $190M-$250M to $8.260B-$8.300B, compared to $15.08 per share and $8.50B in the consensus, respectively.

“This is one of the worst relative performances we’ve seen from ICLR in eons,” Baird analyst Eric Coldwell wrote, downgrading the stock with a $340 per share target. “While we’ve been increasingly cautious on multiple fronts, at minimum we thought the company could protect profit; even that appears questionable,” Coldwell added.

With a gloomy industry outlook indicated in Icon’s (ICLR) results, its rivals have also joined the selloff. Notable decliners IQVIA Holdings (IQV) and Charles River Laboratories (CRL) have yet to report their Q3 results.

data.title: NextEra Partners raised at J.P. Morgan, seeing little downside left after big drop
data.description:
NextEra Energy Partners (NYSE:NEP) -2.9% in Thursday's trading, extending the 16% plunge from the previous session, after posting Q3 results that came in well below expectations.

Seeing little downside remaining after the stock's sharp drop and believing continued ownership by NextEra Energy (NEE) is now a more likely outcome, J.P. Morgan upgrades NextEra Partners (NEP) to Neutral from Underweight with a $22 price target.

JPM's Mark Strouse says near-term risk remains around NextEra Partners' (NEP) longer-term CEPF obligations and its ability to accretively finance potential dropdown acquisitions, while seeing a chance that "NEP can stabilize its cost of capital, grow the portfolio, and perhaps re-establish a virtuous cycle after a likely one-time reset to CAFD and DPU in order to satisfy CEPF obligations."

Strouse also believes a potential dropdown announcement, in conjunction with a DPU reset, could be a catalyst for the stock and provide increased growth visibility into FY 2026 and beyond.

data.title: Upstart expands to super-prime borrowers
data.description:
Upstart Holdings (NASDAQ:UPST) , which has focused its AI lending platform on underserved consumers, is expanding to more affluent borrowers with its new T-Prime lending platform, the company said Thursday.

"With our expansion into T-Prime, we're helping our bank and credit union partners leverage their competitive cost of funding to win the business of our nation’s more affluent borrowers," said co-founder and CEO Dave Girouard.

T-Prime focuses on the almost half of Americans who have credit scores above 720, a demographic that money center banks and fintechs common served.

"By partnering with Upstart in T-Prime, we’re able to offer best-in-class loan options, providing more flexibility, better rates, and broader access, nationwide," said Dennis Devine, president and CEO of Alliant Credit Union.

Upstart (NASDAQ:UPST) stock rose 2.3% in Thursday afternoon trading.

data.title: Baxter to restart hurricane-damaged NC plant next week
data.description:
Baxter (NYSE:BAX) intends to restart its North Cove manufacturing facility in North Carolina, which was knocked offline by Hurricane Helene, by the end of the next week, but is still unsure when production will be restored to pre-hurricane levels.

The company said that staffing at the plant, which was damaged by flooding during the storm, would return to pre-hurricane levels by the end of this week. It hopes to restart production of the highest-throughput IV solutions within next week, according to an update.

“It is important to note that the earliest that new North Cove product could begin entering the distribution channel is mid- to late-November. This is, however, ahead of our original expectations,” Baxter (BAX) said in the update.

The plant is the nation’s largest producer of intravenous and peritoneal dialysis solutions. The temporary closure of the plant has resulted in Baxter (BAX) employing a variety of inventory management and allocation measures to help minimize disruptions to patient care.

Baxter (BAX) noted that the FDA has temporarily authorized the importation of certain products made at its plants in Thailand and Singapore. The agency had already authorized importations from its plants in Ireland, China, Canada, the U.K., Spain and Mexico.

“Our goal remains to return to 90% to 100% allocation of certain IV solution product codes for U.S. customers by the end of 2024,” Baxter (BAX) added.

Baxter (BAX) announced the closure of the North Cove facility, which employs around 2,500 people, on Sept. 29.

The company has said it expects the closure to have a negative impact on its financial results. Baxter (BAX) is expected to release its Q3 earnings report on Nov. 8.

data.title: Nordic Semiconductor ASA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Nordic Semiconductor ASA (OTCPK:NDCVF): Q3 Revenue of USD 159 million, up 18% from Q3 2023.
  • The reported profit before tax was USD 5.5M in Q3 2024.
